blockingIssues.description: Cloudflare fetch-upgrade requests still omit the JS parity header `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK`, so VAL-EXT-001 is not fully met.

`session_input_callback` persistence attribution still matches duplicate-valued callback output against new-turn items before history, diverging from upstream provenance semantics required by VAL-CORE-005.
`MCPServerManager::connect_all()` now clears `active_servers` after a strict connect failure, regressing the upstream behavior that preserves the previous active set.
`docs_contract` portability coverage only rejects `/Users/...` and `\Users\...`, so generic machine-local absolute links can still pass despite VAL-AUDIT-003.

appliedUpdates.description: Added a `docs_contract` command so the shared command manifest exposes the docs contract suite introduced by the audit harness.
Documented that Cloudflare realtime transport parity includes the JS common realtime header bundle, including `User-Agent` and `X-OpenAI-Agents-SDK`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit transcript evidence of a pre-change failing targeted test, or relax the procedure language for audit-only test-addition features where that step is intentionally skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `audit-core-runner-tool-and-handoff-parity` found that the transcript moved from research into edits and only showed targeted test executions after the patch, while the handoff still marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true.
